steps.message:
# Prometheus Metric Naming Convention Enforcement

## Your Task

Find all Go source files in prometheus/prometheus that enforce Prometheus metric naming conventions: the metric name validation (snake_case, unit suffix), the label name validation, and the help text presence check in the client registration.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the compliance domain.

## Available Resources

No local repositories are pre-checked out.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
*(none — all repos available locally)*

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
I'll help you find the Go source files in prometheus/prometheus that enforce metric naming conventions. Let me start by exploring the repository structure and understanding what's available.

    75→// OpenMetricsParser parses samples from a byte slice of samples in the official

    96→	// of the label name and value start and end characters.
    97→	offsets []int

   122→type OpenMetricsOption func(*openMetricsParserOptions)

   124→// WithOMParserSTSeriesSkipped turns off exposing _created lines
   125→// as series, which makes those only used for parsing created timestamp
   126→// for `StartTimestamp` method purposes.
   127→//
   128→// It's recommended to use this option to avoid using _created lines for other
   129→// purposes than created timestamp, but leave false by default for the
   130→// best-effort compatibility.
   131→func WithOMParserSTSeriesSkipped() OpenMetricsOption {
   132→	return func(o *openMetricsParserOptions) {
   133→		o.skipSTSeries = true
   134→	}
   135→}

   138→// in which parser injects __type__ and __unit__ into labels.

   145→// NewOpenMetricsParser returns a new parser for the byte slice with option to skip ST series parsing.
   146→func NewOpenMetricsParser(b []byte, st *labels.SymbolTable, opts ...OpenMetricsOption) Parser {

   151→	}

   153→	parser := &OpenMetricsParser{
   154→		l:                       &openMetricsLexer{b: b},
   155→		builder:                 labels.NewScratchBuilderWithSymbolTable(st, 16),
   156→		skipSTSeries:            options.skipSTSeries,
   157→		enableTypeAndUnitLabels: options.enableTypeAndUnitLabels,
   158→	}
   159→
   160→	return parser
   161→}

   382→	for i := 2; i < len(p.offsets); i += 4 {
   383→		lStart := p.offsets[i] - p.start
   384→		lEnd := p.offsets[i+1] - p.start
   385→		label := p.series[lStart:lEnd]
   386→		// Skip quantile and le labels for summaries and histograms.
   387→		if p.mtype == model.MetricTypeSummary && bytes.Equal(label, quantileBytes) {

   391→			continue

   395→		vEnd := p.offsets[i+3] - p.start
   396→		*offsetsArr = append(*offsetsArr, p.series[vStart:vEnd]...)
   397→	}
   398→
   399→	*offsetsArr = append(*offsetsArr, metricFamilyName...)

   403→	*offsetsArr = (*offsetsArr)[:0]

    14→package schema
    15→
    16→import (
    17→	"github.com/prometheus/common/model"
    18→
    19→	"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/model/labels"
    20→)
    21→
    22→// IsMetadataLabel returns true if the given label name is a special
    23→// schema Metadata label.
    24→func IsMetadataLabel(name string) bool {
    25→	return name == model.MetricNameLabel || name == model.MetricTypeLabel || name == model.MetricUnitLabel
    26→}
    27→
    28→// Metadata represents the core metric schema/metadata elements that:
    29→// * are describing and identifying the metric schema/shape (e.g. name, type and unit).
    30→// * are contributing to the general metric/series identity.
    31→// * with the type-and-unit feature, are stored as Prometheus labels.
    32→//
    33→// Historically, similar information was encoded in the labels.MetricName (suffixes)
    34→// and in the separate metadata.Metadata structures. However, with the
    35→// type-and-unit-label feature (PROM-39), this information can be now stored directly
    36→// in the special schema metadata labels, which offers better reliability (e.g. atomicity),
    37→// compatibility and, in many cases, efficiency.
    38→//
    39→// NOTE: Metadata in the current form is generally similar (yet different) to:
    40→//   - The MetricFamily definition in OpenMetrics (https://prometheus.io/docs/specs/om/open_metrics_spec/#metricfamily).
    41→//     However, there is a small and important distinction around the metric name semantics
    42→//     for the "classic" representation of complex metrics like histograms. The
    43→//     Metadata.Name follows the __name__ semantics. See Name for details.
    44→//   - Original metadata.Metadata entries. However, not all fields in that metadata
    45→//     are "identifiable", notably the help field, plus metadata does not contain Name.
    46→type Metadata struct {
    47→	// Name represents the final metric name for a Prometheus series.
    48→	// NOTE(bwplotka): Prometheus scrape formats (e.g. OpenMetrics) define
    49→	// the "metric family name". The Metadata.Name (so __name__ label) is not
    50→	// always the same as the MetricFamily.Name e.g.:
    51→	// * OpenMetrics metric family name on scrape: "acme_http_router_request_seconds"
    52→	// * Resulting Prometheus metric name: "acme_http_router_request_seconds_sum"
    53→	//
    54→	// Empty string means nameless metric (e.g. result of the PromQL function).
    55→	Name string
    56→	// Type represents the metric type. Empty value ("") is equivalent to
    57→	// model.UnknownMetricType.
    58→	Type model.MetricType
    59→	// Unit represents the metric unit. Empty string means an unitless metric (e.g.
    60→	// result of the PromQL function).
    61→	//
    62→	// NOTE: Currently unit value is not strictly defined other than OpenMetrics
    63→	// recommendations: https://prometheus.io/docs/specs/om/open_metrics_spec/#units-and-base-units
    64→	// TODO(bwplotka): Consider a stricter validation and rules e.g. lowercase only or UCUM standard.
    65→	// Read more in https://github.com/prometheus/proposals/blob/main/proposals/2024-09-25_metadata-labels.md#more-strict-unit-and-type-value-definition
    66→	Unit string
    67→}
    68→
    69→// NewMetadataFromLabels returns the schema metadata from the labels.
    70→func NewMetadataFromLabels(ls labels.Labels) Metadata {
    71→	typ := model.MetricTypeUnknown
    72→	if got := ls.Get(model.MetricTypeLabel); got != "" {
    73→		typ = model.MetricType(got)
    74→	}
    75→	return Metadata{
    76→		Name: ls.Get(model.MetricNameLabel),
    77→		Type: typ,
    78→		Unit: ls.Get(model.MetricUnitLabel),
    79→	}
    80→}
    81→
    82→// IsTypeEmpty returns true if the metric type is empty (not set).
    83→func (m Metadata) IsTypeEmpty() bool {
    84→	return m.Type == "" || m.Type == model.MetricTypeUnknown
    85→}
    86→
    87→// IsEmptyFor returns true if the Metadata field, represented by the given labelName
    88→// is empty (not set). If the labelName in not representing any Metadata field,
    89→// IsEmptyFor returns true.
    90→func (m Metadata) IsEmptyFor(labelName string) bool {
    91→	switch labelName {
    92→	case model.MetricNameLabel:
    93→		return m.Name == ""
    94→	case model.MetricTypeLabel:
    95→		return m.IsTypeEmpty()
    96→	case model.MetricUnitLabel:
    97→		return m.Unit == ""
    98→	default:
    99→		return true
   100→	}
   101→}
   102→
   103→// AddToLabels adds metric schema metadata as labels into the labels.ScratchBuilder.
   104→// Empty Metadata fields will be ignored (not added).
   105→func (m Metadata) AddToLabels(b *labels.ScratchBuilder) {
   106→	if m.Name != "" {
   107→		b.Add(model.MetricNameLabel, m.Name)
   108→	}
   109→	if !m.IsTypeEmpty() {
   110→		b.Add(model.MetricTypeLabel, string(m.Type))
   111→	}
   112→	if m.Unit != "" {
   113→		b.Add(model.MetricUnitLabel, m.Unit)
   114→	}
   115→}
   116→
   117→// SetToLabels injects metric schema metadata as labels into the labels.Builder.
   118→// It follows the labels.Builder.Set semantics, so empty Metadata fields will
   119→// remove the corresponding existing labels if they were previously set.
   120→func (m Metadata) SetToLabels(b *labels.Builder) {
   121→	b.Set(model.MetricNameLabel, m.Name)
   122→	if m.Type == model.MetricTypeUnknown {
   123→		// Unknown equals empty semantically, so remove the label on unknown too as per
   124→		// method signature comment.
   125→		b.Set(model.MetricTypeLabel, "")
   126→	} else {
   127→		b.Set(model.MetricTypeLabel, string(m.Type))
   128→	}
   129→	b.Set(model.MetricUnitLabel, m.Unit)
   130→}
   131→
   132→// NewIgnoreOverriddenMetadataLabelScratchBuilder creates IgnoreOverriddenMetadataLabelScratchBuilder.
   133→func (m Metadata) NewIgnoreOverriddenMetadataLabelScratchBuilder(b *labels.ScratchBuilder) *IgnoreOverriddenMetadataLabelScratchBuilder {
   134→	return &IgnoreOverriddenMetadataLabelScratchBuilder{ScratchBuilder: b, overwrite: m}
   135→}
   136→
   137→// IgnoreOverriddenMetadataLabelScratchBuilder is a wrapper over labels.ScratchBuilder
   138→// that ignores label additions that would collide with non-empty Overwrite Metadata fields.
   139→type IgnoreOverriddenMetadataLabelScratchBuilder struct {
   140→	*labels.ScratchBuilder
   141→	overwrite Metadata
   142→}
   143→
   144→// Add a name/value pair, unless it would collide with the non-empty Overwrite Metadata
   145→// field. Note if you Add the same name twice you will get a duplicate label, which is invalid.
   146→func (b IgnoreOverriddenMetadataLabelScratchBuilder) Add(name, value string) {
   147→	if !b.overwrite.IsEmptyFor(name) {
   148→		return
   149→	}
   150→	b.ScratchBuilder.Add(name, value)
   151→}

    24→func IsMetadataLabel(name string) bool {
    25→	return name == model.MetricNameLabel || name == model.MetricTypeLabel || name == model.MetricUnitLabel

    27→
    28→// Metadata represents the core metric schema/metadata elements that:
    29→// * are describing and identifying the metric schema/shape (e.g. name, type and unit).
    30→// * are contributing to the general metric/series identity.
    31→// * with the type-and-unit feature, are stored as Prometheus labels.
    32→//
    33→// Historically, similar information was encoded in the labels.MetricName (suffixes)
    34→// and in the separate metadata.Metadata structures. However, with the
    35→// type-and-unit-label feature (PROM-39), this information can be now stored directly
    36→// in the special schema metadata labels, which offers better reliability (e.g. atomicity),
    37→// compatibility and, in many cases, efficiency.
    38→//
    39→// NOTE: Metadata in the current form is generally similar (yet different) to:
    40→//   - The MetricFamily definition in OpenMetrics (https://prometheus.io/docs/specs/om/open_metrics_spec/#metricfamily).
    41→//     However, there is a small and important distinction around the metric name semantics
    42→//     for the "classic" representation of complex metrics like histograms. The
    43→//     Metadata.Name follows the __name__ semantics. See Name for details.
    44→//   - Original metadata.Metadata entries. However, not all fields in that metadata
    45→//     are "identifiable", notably the help field, plus metadata does not contain Name.
    46→type Metadata struct {
    47→	// Name represents the final metric name for a Prometheus series.
    48→	// NOTE(bwplotka): Prometheus scrape formats (e.g. OpenMetrics) define
    49→	// the "metric family name". The Metadata.Name (so __name__ label) is not
    50→	// always the same as the MetricFamily.Name e.g.:
    51→	// * OpenMetrics metric family name on scrape: "acme_http_router_request_seconds"
    52→	// * Resulting Prometheus metric name: "acme_http_router_request_seconds_sum"
    53→	//
    54→	// Empty string means nameless metric (e.g. result of the PromQL function).
    55→	Name string
    56→	// Type represents the metric type. Empty value ("") is equivalent to
    57→	// model.UnknownMetricType.
    58→	Type model.MetricType
    59→	// Unit represents the metric unit. Empty string means an unitless metric (e.g.
    60→	// result of the PromQL function).
    61→	//
    62→	// NOTE: Currently unit value is not strictly defined other than OpenMetrics
    63→	// recommendations: https://prometheus.io/docs/specs/om/open_metrics_spec/#units-and-base-units
    64→	// TODO(bwplotka): Consider a stricter validation and rules e.g. lowercase only or UCUM standard.
